# DevAssist

### Autonomous Multi-Agent AI Software Engineering Platform

<p>

**AI-powered Repository Intelligence • Code Understanding • Documentation • Architecture • Code Review • Semantic Search**

</p>

<p>

Build enterprise-grade software faster with autonomous AI agents capable of understanding, analyzing, documenting, reviewing, and explaining entire GitHub repositories.

Powered by **LangGraph**, **LangChain**, **Groq**, **React**, **TypeScript**, and **RAG**.

</p>

# About DevAssist

**DevAssist** is an enterprise-grade AI Software Engineering Platform that combines **Large Language Models (LLMs)**, **Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)**, **LangGraph**, and **Multi-Agent AI** to help developers understand, analyze, document, review, and maintain software repositories with minimal manual effort.

Instead of acting as a traditional chatbot, DevAssist functions as an intelligent software engineering assistant capable of reasoning across an entire repository. It understands relationships between files, interprets project architecture, performs semantic code search, generates documentation, identifies security issues, reviews pull requests, and answers repository-specific questions using contextual retrieval.

The platform is designed to accelerate software development workflows, improve code quality, reduce onboarding time, and provide engineering teams with AI-powered insights throughout the software development lifecycle.

Whether working on a small open-source project or a large enterprise codebase, DevAssist provides developers with a centralized AI workspace for repository intelligence.

---

# Problem Statement

Modern software repositories are growing larger and more complex every year.

Engineering teams frequently struggle with:

- Understanding unfamiliar codebases
- Reading thousands of source files
- Navigating deeply nested project structures
- Keeping documentation synchronized with code
- Reviewing pull requests efficiently
- Identifying security vulnerabilities
- Performing semantic code searches
- Onboarding new developers
- Understanding system architecture
- Maintaining consistent coding standards

Traditional code search tools rely heavily on keyword matching and static indexing, making them insufficient for understanding the true semantic relationships within large software projects.

As repositories scale, these challenges lead to:

- Increased onboarding time
- Reduced developer productivity
- Higher maintenance costs
- Poor documentation quality
- Technical debt accumulation
- Security risks
- Inefficient collaboration

---

# Solution Overview

DevAssist addresses these challenges through an autonomous AI-powered repository intelligence platform.

The platform automatically:

- Imports repositories from GitHub
- Clones project source code
- Parses every source file
- Generates semantic embeddings
- Builds an AI-searchable knowledge base
- Indexes repository metadata
- Understands project architecture
- Creates documentation automatically
- Generates README files
- Reviews source code
- Detects security issues
- Produces architecture diagrams
- Answers repository questions using RAG
- Coordinates specialized AI agents through LangGraph

Instead of manually exploring hundreds of files, developers can simply ask natural language questions and receive accurate, context-aware answers generated directly from their codebase.

---

# Project Vision

Our vision is to build the next generation of AI-powered software engineering assistants capable of acting as intelligent collaborators rather than simple code generators.

DevAssist aims to become an autonomous engineering platform capable of:

- Understanding complete software systems
- Assisting developers throughout the SDLC
- Reducing repetitive engineering tasks
- Improving software quality automatically
- Accelerating onboarding
- Enhancing collaboration
- Providing actionable engineering insights
- Automating documentation
- Assisting code reviews
- Supporting enterprise-scale software development

The long-term objective is to transform software repositories into interactive, AI-powered knowledge systems that continuously evolve alongside the codebase.

---

# Why DevAssist?

Unlike traditional AI coding assistants that focus primarily on code completion, DevAssist analyzes and understands entire repositories.

### Traditional Development Workflow

```
Developer

↓

Read Documentation

↓

Search Files

↓

Open Multiple Files

↓

Understand Architecture

↓

Analyze Dependencies

↓

Review Code

↓

Write Documentation
```

---

### DevAssist Workflow

```
Developer

↓

Ask Question

↓

DevAssist

↓

Repository Understanding

↓

Multi-Agent Analysis

↓

AI Reasoning

↓

Accurate Response
```

---

## What Makes DevAssist Different?

| Traditional Tools | DevAssist |
|-------------------|-----------|
| Keyword Search | Semantic Repository Search |
| File-Level Context | Repository-Level Context |
| Manual Documentation | AI Documentation Generation |
| Manual Code Reviews | AI Code Review |
| Static Architecture | AI Architecture Generation |
| Single AI Model | Multi-Agent System |
| Basic Chatbot | Context-Aware Repository Assistant |
| Manual Analysis | Autonomous AI Workflows |
| Generic Responses | Repository-Specific Intelligence |

---

# Core Capabilities

DevAssist provides a complete suite of AI-powered software engineering capabilities.

| Capability | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| AI Repository Chat | Ask repository-specific questions using natural language |
| GitHub Integration | Import public and private repositories |
| Documentation Generator | Automatically generate project documentation |
| README Generator | Create professional README files |
| Architecture Generator | Generate Mermaid architecture diagrams |
| Semantic Search | Search repositories using meaning instead of keywords |
| Security Analysis | Detect vulnerabilities and security risks |
| Dependency Analysis | Understand module relationships |
| Repository Analytics | Visualize repository insights |
| Multi-Agent AI | Specialized AI agents collaborate autonomously |
| Project Insights | Repository health and engineering metrics |
| AI Code Review | Automated code quality evaluation |

# Project Goals

The primary objectives of DevAssist are:

- Simplify repository understanding
- Improve developer productivity
- Reduce onboarding time
- Automate documentation
- Increase software quality
- Improve engineering collaboration
- Enable AI-assisted software engineering
- Build an extensible multi-agent ecosystem
- Provide enterprise-ready repository intelligence

---

# Target Users

DevAssist is designed for:

- Software Engineers
- Full Stack Developers
- Backend Developers
- Frontend Developers
- DevOps Engineers
- Platform Engineers
- Solution Architects
- Technical Leads
- Engineering Managers
- Open Source Contributors
- Startup Teams
- Enterprise Development Teams
- Students learning large codebases
- AI-assisted software engineering researchers

---

---

# Architecture

DevAssist follows a modular, AI-first architecture designed to analyze, understand, and reason over complete software repositories. The platform combines modern web technologies with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), vector search, and autonomous multi-agent orchestration to deliver repository-aware AI assistance.

The architecture is designed around the following principles:

- **Modularity** – Each subsystem is isolated and independently scalable.
- **Extensibility** – New AI agents and services can be integrated without changing the core architecture.
- **Parallel Processing** – Independent AI tasks execute concurrently using LangGraph.
- **Context Awareness** – Every AI response is grounded in repository-specific knowledge retrieved from vector embeddings.
- **Developer Experience** – A responsive React dashboard provides real-time visibility into repository processing and AI execution.

---

# High-Level System Architecture

The following diagram illustrates the major components of DevAssist and how they interact.

```mermaid
flowchart LR

User([Developer])

subgraph Frontend
UI[React Dashboard]
Explorer[Repository Explorer]
Chat[AI Repository Chat]
Analytics[Analytics Dashboard]
end

subgraph Backend
API[Express API]
Auth[Authentication]
RepoService[Repository Service]
SearchService[Search Service]
AIService[AI Service]
end

subgraph AI
Orchestrator[LangGraph Orchestrator]

ArchAgent[Architecture Agent]
DocAgent[Documentation Agent]
ReadmeAgent[README Agent]
ReviewAgent[Code Review Agent]
SecurityAgent[Security Agent]
SearchAgent[Semantic Search Agent]
end

subgraph Data
SQLite[(SQLite)]
VectorDB[(Vector Store)]
Embeddings[(Embeddings)]
end

subgraph External
GitHub[GitHub API]
Groq[Groq LLM]
end

User --> UI

UI --> API

API --> Auth
API --> RepoService
API --> SearchService
API --> AIService

RepoService --> GitHub

RepoService --> SQLite

SearchService --> VectorDB

AIService --> Orchestrator

Orchestrator --> ArchAgent
Orchestrator --> DocAgent
Orchestrator --> ReadmeAgent
Orchestrator --> ReviewAgent
Orchestrator --> SecurityAgent
Orchestrator --> SearchAgent

ArchAgent --> Groq
DocAgent --> Groq
ReadmeAgent --> Groq
ReviewAgent --> Groq
SecurityAgent --> Groq
SearchAgent --> Groq

VectorDB --> Embeddings

SQLite --> API

VectorDB --> AIService
```

---

# Component Architecture

DevAssist is divided into several independent modules that communicate through well-defined interfaces.

| Layer | Responsibility |
|---------|----------------|
| Frontend | Dashboard, Repository Explorer, AI Chat, Analytics |
| API Layer | Authentication, REST APIs, Repository Management |
| Service Layer | Business Logic and Repository Processing |
| AI Layer | LangGraph Multi-Agent Orchestration |
| Knowledge Layer | Vector Embeddings and Semantic Search |
| Data Layer | SQLite Database and Repository Metadata |
| External Services | GitHub API and Groq LLM |

---

# Layered Architecture

The application follows a layered architecture to separate concerns and improve maintainability.

```mermaid
flowchart TD

Presentation["Presentation Layer
React
TypeScript
Tailwind CSS"]

API["API Layer
Express
REST API
JWT"]

Service["Service Layer
Repository Services
AI Services
Search Services"]

AI["AI Layer
LangGraph
LLMs
Agents"]

Knowledge["Knowledge Layer
Embeddings
Vector Database
Retriever"]

Database["Data Layer
SQLite
Repository Metadata"]

Infrastructure["Infrastructure
GitHub
Groq
Environment"]

Presentation --> API

API --> Service

Service --> AI

AI --> Knowledge

Knowledge --> Database

AI --> Infrastructure
```

---

# Core Components

## Frontend

The frontend provides an enterprise-grade developer experience through an interactive dashboard.

Major responsibilities include:

- Repository Explorer
- AI Chat
- Documentation Viewer
- Analytics Dashboard
- Mermaid Diagram Rendering
- Real-time Agent Status
- Processing Progress
- Authentication
- Responsive UI

Technology Stack

- React 19
- TypeScript
- Tailwind CSS
- Zustand
- React Query
- Framer Motion
- Mermaid.js

---

## Backend

The backend coordinates every operation inside DevAssist.

Responsibilities include:

- REST APIs
- Authentication
- Repository Import
- Repository Cloning
- Repository Parsing
- AI Request Routing
- Search APIs
- Documentation APIs
- Analytics APIs

Technology Stack

- Node.js
- Express
- TypeScript

---

## AI Layer

The AI Layer is responsible for repository reasoning.

Instead of sending one large prompt to an LLM, DevAssist distributes work among specialized AI agents coordinated by LangGraph.

Responsibilities include:

- Repository Understanding
- Architecture Analysis
- Documentation Generation
- README Generation
- Code Review
- Security Review
- Dependency Analysis
- Semantic Search

---

## Knowledge Layer

The Knowledge Layer powers Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).

Every repository is transformed into searchable semantic embeddings.

Responsibilities:

- File Chunking
- Embedding Generation
- Vector Storage
- Similarity Search
- Context Retrieval
- Metadata Indexing

---

## Data Layer

Repository metadata is stored separately from vector embeddings.

SQLite stores:

- Users
- Repositories
- Chats
- Processing Status
- Agent Tasks
- Analytics
- Repository Metadata

The Vector Database stores:

- File Embeddings
- Chunk Embeddings
- Search Indexes
- Semantic Relationships

---

# Architecture Principles

DevAssist is built around the following engineering principles.

### Modular

Every subsystem is independently replaceable.

---

### Scalable

New AI agents can be introduced without modifying existing workflows.

---

### Parallel

Independent AI tasks execute simultaneously through LangGraph.

---

### Repository-Aware

Every AI response is grounded using Retrieval-Augmented Generation.

---

### Extensible

The platform is designed to support:

- Additional AI models
- Custom agents
- Multiple vector databases
- Enterprise authentication providers
- Cloud-native deployments

---

# Architecture Summary

| Category | Technology |
|------------|------------|
| Frontend | React + TypeScript |
| Backend | Node.js + Express |
| AI Framework | LangGraph |
| LLM | Groq |
| Knowledge Base | Vector Embeddings |
| Database | SQLite |
| Search | Semantic Vector Search |
| Authentication | JWT |
| Documentation | AI Generated |
| Architecture | AI Generated |
| Deployment | Docker Ready |

---

---

# Overall Application Workflow

The following diagram illustrates the complete lifecycle of a repository from import to AI-powered insights.

```mermaid
flowchart LR

User([Developer])

GitHub[(GitHub Repository)]

Import[Import Repository]

Clone[Clone Repository]

Parse[Parse Files]

Chunk[Chunk Source Code]

Embed[Generate Embeddings]

Vector[(Vector Database)]

Index[Index Metadata]

LangGraph[LangGraph Orchestrator]

Agents[AI Agents]

Dashboard[Dashboard]

User --> Import

GitHub --> Import

Import --> Clone

Clone --> Parse

Parse --> Chunk

Chunk --> Embed

Embed --> Vector

Parse --> Index

Vector --> LangGraph

Index --> LangGraph

LangGraph --> Agents

Agents --> Dashboard

Dashboard --> User
```

---

# Repository Import Workflow

DevAssist supports importing repositories from GitHub using Personal Access Tokens or public repository URLs.

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram

actor User

participant UI as Dashboard

participant API

participant GitHub

participant DB

User->>UI: Import Repository

UI->>API: POST /repositories

API->>GitHub: Validate Repository

GitHub-->>API: Repository Metadata

API->>GitHub: Clone Repository

GitHub-->>API: Repository Files

API->>DB: Save Metadata

DB-->>API: Success

API-->>UI: Repository Imported

UI-->>User: Display Repository
```

---

# Repository Processing Pipeline

Once imported, the repository moves through several processing stages before becoming AI-ready.

```mermaid
flowchart LR

Repository

-->

Clone

-->

FileDiscovery

-->

LanguageDetection

-->

ASTParsing

-->

DependencyAnalysis

-->

Chunking

-->

EmbeddingGeneration

-->

VectorStorage

-->

RepositoryReady
```

---

# File Parsing Workflow

Every source file is analyzed independently.

```mermaid
flowchart TD

Repository

-->

ReadFiles

-->

IdentifyLanguage

-->

ExtractMetadata

-->

ParseSyntax

-->

ExtractImports

-->

ExtractFunctions

-->

ExtractClasses

-->

ExtractComments

-->

StructuredDocument
```

---

# Chunking Pipeline

Large files are split into semantically meaningful chunks.

```mermaid
flowchart LR

SourceFile

-->

Tokenizer

-->

SmartChunker

-->

OverlapHandler

-->

ChunkValidation

-->

ChunkMetadata

-->

EmbeddingQueue
```

---

# Embedding Generation Pipeline

Each chunk is transformed into a vector representation for semantic retrieval.

```mermaid
flowchart LR

Chunk

-->

EmbeddingModel

-->

VectorGeneration

-->

MetadataAttachment

-->

VectorDatabase

-->

SimilarityIndex
```

---

# Repository Indexing Workflow

Repository metadata and embeddings are indexed separately for efficient retrieval.

```mermaid
flowchart TD

Repository

-->

Metadata

-->

SQLite

Repository

-->

Chunks

-->

Embeddings

-->

VectorDB

SQLite --> SearchEngine

VectorDB --> SearchEngine

SearchEngine --> RepositoryKnowledgeBase
```

---

# Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) Architecture

Every AI response is grounded in repository context using Retrieval-Augmented Generation.

```mermaid
flowchart LR

Question

-->

Embedding

-->

VectorSearch

-->

TopKChunks

-->

ContextBuilder

-->

PromptBuilder

-->

GroqLLM

-->

FinalAnswer
```

---

# AI Repository Chat Workflow

Natural language questions are answered using repository-specific knowledge.

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram

actor User

participant Chat

participant API

participant Retriever

participant Groq

User->>Chat: Ask Question

Chat->>API: Query

API->>Retriever: Semantic Search

Retriever-->>API: Relevant Chunks

API->>Groq: Context + Question

Groq-->>API: AI Response

API-->>Chat: Answer

Chat-->>User: Display Response
```

---

# Semantic Search Workflow

Unlike keyword search, DevAssist searches by meaning.

```mermaid
flowchart LR

UserQuery

-->

Embedding

-->

VectorSearch

-->

SimilarityRanking

-->

ContextRetrieval

-->

LLMReasoning

-->

SearchResults
```

---

# Repository Processing Stages

| Stage | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| Import | Repository registration |
| Clone | Download repository |
| Parse | Read source code |
| Analyze | Extract structure |
| Chunk | Split into semantic units |
| Embed | Generate vector embeddings |
| Index | Store searchable metadata |
| Retrieve | Perform semantic retrieval |
| Reason | AI reasoning with LangGraph |
| Respond | Generate contextual response |

---

# Pipeline Advantages

Repository-aware AI responses

Semantic understanding instead of keyword matching

Fast vector retrieval

Parallel repository indexing

Intelligent chunking strategy

Optimized context retrieval

Scalable knowledge base

Accurate AI reasoning

---

---

# Multi-Agent AI Architecture

DevAssist is powered by an autonomous **Multi-Agent AI System** built on **LangGraph**. Instead of relying on a single LLM prompt, the platform delegates repository analysis to specialized AI agents that execute independently and collaborate through a centralized orchestrator.

Each agent has a focused responsibility, allowing the system to perform complex repository analysis efficiently while maintaining modularity and scalability.

---

# LangGraph Orchestrator

The LangGraph Orchestrator serves as the central brain of the platform. It coordinates task execution, routes repository context to the appropriate AI agents, manages dependencies between tasks, and aggregates results into a unified response.

```mermaid
flowchart TD

Start([Repository Ready])

Start --> Planner

Planner[LangGraph Orchestrator]

Planner --> Documentation

Planner --> Readme

Planner --> Architecture

Planner --> Review

Planner --> Security

Planner --> Dependency

Planner --> Analytics

Documentation --> Merge

Readme --> Merge

Architecture --> Merge

Review --> Merge

Security --> Merge

Dependency --> Merge

Analytics --> Merge

Merge[Result Aggregator]

Merge --> Dashboard

Dashboard --> User
```

---

# AI Agent Responsibilities

| Agent | Responsibility |
|---------|---------------|
| Documentation Agent | Generates technical documentation |
| README Agent | Creates professional README.md files |
| Architecture Agent | Generates architecture diagrams and system documentation |
| Search Agent | Handles semantic repository search |
| Security Agent | Detects vulnerabilities and insecure code |
| Dependency Agent | Builds dependency graphs |
| Code Review Agent | Reviews source code quality |
| Analytics Agent | Produces repository insights |
| Knowledge Agent | Builds repository knowledge base |

---

# Multi-Agent Execution Workflow

The repository is analyzed in parallel by independent AI agents.

```mermaid
flowchart LR

Repository

-->

LangGraph

LangGraph --> Documentation

LangGraph --> Review

LangGraph --> Security

LangGraph --> Architecture

LangGraph --> Readme

LangGraph --> Search

LangGraph --> Analytics

Documentation --> Response

Review --> Response

Security --> Response

Architecture --> Response

Readme --> Response

Search --> Response

Analytics --> Response

Response --> Dashboard
```

---

# Documentation Generation Workflow

The Documentation Agent automatically creates project documentation by analyzing repository structure, modules, APIs, and comments.

```mermaid
flowchart TD

Repository

-->

ParseSource

-->

ExtractMetadata

-->

IdentifyModules

-->

SummarizeFiles

-->

GenerateMarkdown

-->

Documentation
```

---

# README Generation Workflow

The README Agent generates a complete README.md using repository metadata and AI-generated insights.

```mermaid
flowchart LR

Repository

-->

Metadata

-->

ProjectAnalysis

-->

FeatureExtraction

-->

ArchitectureSummary

-->

MarkdownGeneration

-->

README
```

---

# Architecture Generation Workflow

The Architecture Agent identifies relationships between modules and generates visual system diagrams.

```mermaid
flowchart TD

Repository

-->

DependencyGraph

-->

ModuleAnalysis

-->

RelationshipDetection

-->

MermaidGenerator

-->

ArchitectureDiagram
```

---

# AI Code Review Workflow

The Code Review Agent evaluates code quality, maintainability, complexity, and best practices.

```mermaid
flowchart LR

SourceCode

-->

StaticAnalysis

-->

CodeQuality

-->

BestPractices

-->

BugDetection

-->

Suggestions

-->

ReviewReport
```

---

# Security Analysis Workflow

The Security Agent scans repositories for vulnerabilities, insecure dependencies, exposed secrets, and OWASP risks.

```mermaid
flowchart TD

Repository

-->

DependencyScan

-->

SecretDetection

-->

StaticSecurityAnalysis

-->

OWASPChecks

-->

RiskAssessment

-->

SecurityReport
```

---

# Dependency Analysis Workflow

The Dependency Agent constructs an internal dependency graph for better repository understanding.

```mermaid
flowchart LR

Repository

-->

ImportDetection

-->

PackageAnalysis

-->

DependencyTree

-->

CircularDependencyCheck

-->

DependencyGraph
```

---

# Analytics Generation Workflow

The Analytics Agent produces engineering insights about the repository.

```mermaid
flowchart TD

Repository

-->

CollectMetrics

-->

LOCAnalysis

-->

ComplexityAnalysis

-->

LanguageStatistics

-->

RepositoryHealth

-->

DashboardMetrics
```

---

# AI Response Generation

After every AI agent completes its task, LangGraph merges all outputs into a single coherent response.

```mermaid
flowchart LR

Documentation

-->

Aggregator

Architecture

-->

Aggregator

Review

-->

Aggregator

Security

-->

Aggregator

Analytics

-->

Aggregator

Search

-->

Aggregator

Aggregator

-->

PromptBuilder

-->

GroqLLM

-->

FinalResponse
```

---

# Agent Communication Flow

Agents communicate through shared repository state managed by LangGraph.

```mermaid
flowchart TD

RepositoryState

-->

DocumentationAgent

RepositoryState

-->

ReviewAgent

RepositoryState

-->

ArchitectureAgent

RepositoryState

-->

SecurityAgent

RepositoryState

-->

AnalyticsAgent

DocumentationAgent --> SharedState

ReviewAgent --> SharedState

ArchitectureAgent --> SharedState

SecurityAgent --> SharedState

AnalyticsAgent --> SharedState

SharedState --> FinalResponse
```

---

# Parallel Execution Model

Independent AI agents execute simultaneously, reducing repository processing time.

```mermaid
flowchart LR

Repository

-->

LangGraph

LangGraph --> Agent1

LangGraph --> Agent2

LangGraph --> Agent3

LangGraph --> Agent4

LangGraph --> Agent5

LangGraph --> Agent6

LangGraph --> Agent7

Agent1 --> Finish

Agent2 --> Finish

Agent3 --> Finish

Agent4 --> Finish

Agent5 --> Finish

Agent6 --> Finish

Agent7 --> Finish
```

---

# Multi-Agent Benefits

| Feature | Benefit |
|----------|---------|
| Parallel Execution | Faster repository analysis |
| Modular Agents | Easy extensibility |
| Shared State | Consistent repository understanding |
| Specialized Prompts | Higher quality outputs |
| Independent Workflows | Better scalability |
| Result Aggregation | Unified AI responses |
| LangGraph DAG | Reliable execution flow |
| Context Sharing | Repository-aware intelligence |

---

# AI Processing Pipeline Summary

```text
Repository
      │
      ▼
Repository Parser
      │
      ▼
Knowledge Base
      │
      ▼
LangGraph Orchestrator
      │
 ┌────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┐
 │    │      │       │       │       │          │
 ▼    ▼      ▼       ▼       ▼       ▼          ▼
Docs README Review Security Search Analytics Architecture
 │    │      │       │       │       │          │
 └────────────── Result Aggregation ─────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
                Groq LLM
                     │
                     ▼
             AI Repository Assistant
```

---

# Database Architecture

DevAssist separates structured metadata from semantic knowledge. Repository metadata is stored in a relational database, while embeddings are stored in a vector database for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).

```mermaid
erDiagram

USER ||--o{ REPOSITORY : owns

REPOSITORY ||--o{ FILE : contains

REPOSITORY ||--o{ CHAT : has

REPOSITORY ||--o{ AGENT_TASK : executes

REPOSITORY ||--o{ ANALYTICS : generates

FILE ||--o{ EMBEDDING : indexed_as

CHAT ||--o{ MESSAGE : contains

USER {

int id

string name

string email

string password

datetime createdAt

}

REPOSITORY {

int id

string githubUrl

string branch

string status

datetime importedAt

}

FILE {

int id

string path

string language

int repositoryId

}

EMBEDDING {

int id

string chunkId

vector embedding

}

CHAT {

int id

int repositoryId

}

MESSAGE {

int id

string role

text content

}

AGENT_TASK {

int id

string agent

string status

}

ANALYTICS {

int id

string metric

string value

}
```

---

# Database Schema Overview

| Table | Purpose |
|---------|----------|
| Users | Authentication |
| Repositories | Imported repositories |
| Files | Source code metadata |
| Embeddings | Vector embeddings |
| Chats | AI conversations |
| Messages | Chat history |
| Agent Tasks | LangGraph execution |
| Analytics | Repository insights |

---

# REST API Architecture

The frontend communicates exclusively through the Express REST API.

```mermaid
flowchart LR

Browser

-->

ExpressAPI

ExpressAPI --> Authentication

ExpressAPI --> RepositoryService

ExpressAPI --> AIService

ExpressAPI --> SearchService

ExpressAPI --> AnalyticsService

RepositoryService --> SQLite

SearchService --> VectorDB

AIService --> LangGraph

LangGraph --> Groq

AnalyticsService --> SQLite
```

---

# API Request Lifecycle

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram

actor User

participant React

participant API

participant Auth

participant Service

participant Database

User->>React: Click Action

React->>API: HTTP Request

API->>Auth: Validate JWT

Auth-->>API: Authorized

API->>Service: Execute Request

Service->>Database: Query Data

Database-->>Service: Result

Service-->>API: Response

API-->>React: JSON

React-->>User: Update UI
```

---

# Authentication Flow

DevAssist secures every API request using JWT-based authentication.

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram

actor User

participant Frontend

participant Backend

participant JWT

User->>Frontend: Login

Frontend->>Backend: Credentials

Backend->>JWT: Generate Token

JWT-->>Backend: Access Token

Backend-->>Frontend: JWT

Frontend->>Backend: Protected Request

Backend->>JWT: Verify Token

JWT-->>Backend: Valid

Backend-->>Frontend: Protected Response
```

---

# Authorization Workflow

```mermaid
flowchart TD

Request

-->

JWTValidation

JWTValidation --> Valid

JWTValidation --> Invalid

Valid --> UserContext

UserContext --> RoleCheck

RoleCheck --> Authorized

RoleCheck --> Unauthorized

Authorized --> ExecuteAPI

Unauthorized --> Error403

Invalid --> Error401
```

---

# Repository Processing API

```mermaid
flowchart LR

Client --> PostImport[POST Repository Import] --> RepoService[Repository Service] --> CloneRepo[Clone Repository] --> RepoParser[Repository Parser] --> EmbedService[Embedding Service] --> LangGraph --> Ready[Repository Ready]
```

---

# Deployment Architecture

DevAssist is designed for cloud-native deployment.

```mermaid
flowchart LR

User

-->

Browser

-->

Nginx

-->

ReactFrontend

-->

ExpressBackend

ExpressBackend --> SQLite

ExpressBackend --> VectorDB

ExpressBackend --> LangGraph

LangGraph --> GroqAPI

ExpressBackend --> GitHubAPI
```

---

# Docker Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TD

DockerCompose

-->

FrontendContainer

DockerCompose

-->

BackendContainer

DockerCompose

-->

SQLiteVolume

DockerCompose

-->

VectorDB

BackendContainer --> SQLiteVolume

BackendContainer --> VectorDB

FrontendContainer --> BackendContainer
```

---

# CI/CD Pipeline

Every commit automatically triggers testing, building, and deployment.

```mermaid
flowchart LR

Developer

-->

GitHub

-->

GitHubActions

GitHubActions --> Install

Install --> Lint

Lint --> Test

Test --> Build

Build --> Docker

Docker --> Deploy

Deploy --> Production
```

---

# Production Infrastructure

```mermaid
flowchart TD

Internet

-->

LoadBalancer

-->

Frontend

Frontend --> API

API --> Database

API --> VectorDB

API --> LangGraph

LangGraph --> Groq

API --> GitHub
```

---

# Monitoring Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart LR

Application

-->

Logs

Application

-->

Metrics

Application

-->

HealthChecks

Logs --> Dashboard

Metrics --> Dashboard

HealthChecks --> Dashboard

Dashboard --> Admin
```

---

# Performance Optimization Pipeline

```mermaid
flowchart LR

Request

-->

CacheCheck

CacheCheck --> CacheHit

CacheCheck --> CacheMiss

CacheMiss --> VectorSearch

VectorSearch --> Groq

Groq --> Response

CacheHit --> Response
```

---

# Security Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TD

User

-->

HTTPS

-->

JWTAuthentication

-->

Authorization

-->

InputValidation

-->

RateLimiter

-->

BusinessLogic

-->

Database
```

---

# Project Structure

```text
devassist/

├── dashboard/
│   ├── components/
│   ├── pages/
│   ├── hooks/
│   ├── stores/
│   ├── services/
│   └── layouts/
│
├── api/
│   ├── controllers/
│   ├── routes/
│   ├── middlewares/
│   ├── validators/
│   └── services/
│
├── orchestrator/
│   ├── graph/
│   ├── nodes/
│   ├── edges/
│   ├── state/
│   └── workflows/
│
├── agents/
│   ├── documentation/
│   ├── readme/
│   ├── architecture/
│   ├── security/
│   ├── review/
│   ├── analytics/
│   └── search/
│
├── core/
│   ├── database/
│   ├── embeddings/
│   ├── github/
│   ├── parser/
│   ├── chunker/
│   └── utils/
│
├── docs/
├── docker/
├── tests/
├── scripts/
└── server.ts
```

---

# Infrastructure Summary

| Component | Technology |
|------------|------------|
| Frontend | React + TypeScript |
| Backend | Express + Node.js |
| AI Framework | LangGraph |
| LLM | Groq |
| Authentication | JWT |
| Database | SQLite |
| Vector Store | Chroma / Pinecone / FAISS |
| Documentation | Markdown |
| Containerization | Docker |
| Reverse Proxy | Nginx |
| CI/CD | GitHub Actions |
| Monitoring | Prometheus / Grafana |

---

# Architecture Highlights

- Enterprise-ready layered architecture
- Modular AI agent system
- Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
- Parallel LangGraph execution
- Repository-aware semantic search
- JWT-based authentication
- Cloud-native deployment
- Docker-ready infrastructure
- CI/CD automation
- Scalable and extensible design


---

# Core Features

DevAssist combines Artificial Intelligence, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), Semantic Search, and Multi-Agent orchestration into a unified software engineering platform.

The platform consists of several intelligent modules working together to automate repository understanding, documentation, architecture generation, code reviews, and software analysis.

---

# AI Repository Chat

## Overview

The AI Repository Chat enables developers to interact with an entire codebase using natural language. Instead of manually searching through hundreds of files, users can simply ask questions such as:

- Explain this project
- How does authentication work?
- Where is JWT implemented?
- Show database relationships
- Explain API architecture
- Which files use LangGraph?
- Find all security middleware.

The assistant retrieves only the most relevant repository context before generating responses using Groq LLMs.

---

## Features

- Repository-aware conversations
- Contextual answers
- Code explanation
- Architecture explanation
- API understanding
- Dependency reasoning
- Cross-file understanding
- Conversation history

---

## Workflow

```mermaid
flowchart LR

Question --> Embedding --> VectorSearch[Vector Search] --> RelevantFiles[Relevant Files] --> ContextBuilder[Context Builder] --> GroqLLM[Groq LLM] --> Answer
```

---

**Screenshot Placeholder**

```
docs/screenshots/ai-chat.png
```

---

# GitHub Repository Import

## Overview

Import public or private GitHub repositories directly into DevAssist.

The platform automatically:

- validates the repository
- clones the repository
- indexes the project
- generates embeddings
- prepares the repository for AI analysis

---

## Supported

- Public repositories

- Private repositories

- GitHub Personal Access Tokens

- Branch selection

- Re-import repositories

- Incremental indexing

---

## Workflow

```mermaid
flowchart LR

GitHub --> Validate[Repository Validation] --> Clone[Clone Repository] --> SaveMeta[Save Metadata] --> Ready[Repository Ready]
```

---

Screenshot

```
docs/screenshots/import.png
```

---

# Repository Explorer

Browse the repository structure directly inside the dashboard.

Features include:

- Tree navigation

- Folder expansion

- File preview

- Syntax highlighting

- Language detection

- Search

- File metadata

---

Workflow

```mermaid
flowchart LR

Repository --> TreeBuilder[Tree Builder] --> Explorer --> Developer
```

---

Screenshot

```
docs/screenshots/explorer.png
```

---

# Semantic Repository Search

Unlike keyword search, semantic search understands the intent behind your queries.

Example searches:

> Authentication middleware

> Payment implementation

> JWT validation

> Error handling

> AI agents

> Database schema

The search engine retrieves semantically similar code instead of exact text matches.

---

Capabilities

- Vector Search

- Similarity Search

- Metadata Filtering

- Context Retrieval

- File Ranking

---

Workflow

```mermaid
flowchart LR

Query --> Embedding --> VectorSearch[Vector Search] --> Ranking --> Context --> Results
```

---

Screenshot

```
docs/screenshots/search.png
```

---

# AI Documentation Generator

Automatically generates technical documentation for repositories.

Generated documentation includes:

- Module Documentation

- API Documentation

- Folder Documentation

- Class Documentation

- Function Documentation

- Dependency Documentation

---

Workflow

```mermaid
flowchart TD

Repository --> Analyze --> Summarize --> MarkdownGen[Markdown Generator] --> Documentation
```

---

Screenshot

```
docs/screenshots/documentation.png
```

---

# AI README Generator

Generate professional README files for any repository.

Generated sections include:

- Installation

- Features

- Tech Stack

- Architecture

- Usage

- License

- Contribution Guide

- API Reference

- Screenshots

---

Workflow

```mermaid
flowchart TD

Repository --> Analysis[Repository Analysis] --> MarkdownGen[Markdown Generator] --> Readme[README.md]
```

---

Screenshot

```
docs/screenshots/readme.png
```

---

# AI Architecture Generator

Automatically generates architecture documentation and Mermaid diagrams.

Supported diagrams:

- System Architecture

- Component Diagram

- Sequence Diagram

- Flowchart

- Entity Relationship Diagram

- Deployment Diagram

- Workflow Diagram

---

Workflow

```mermaid
flowchart LR

Repository --> DepGraph[Dependency Graph] --> ArchAnalysis[Architecture Analysis] --> MermaidGen[Mermaid Generator] --> Diagram
```

---

Screenshot

```
docs/screenshots/architecture.png
```

---

# AI Code Review

Analyze repositories for:

- Code Smells

- Bugs

- Maintainability

- Best Practices

- Complexity

- Duplicate Code

- Dead Code

- Naming Issues

- Performance Problems

---

Workflow

```mermaid
flowchart LR

SourceCode[Source Code] --> StaticAnalysis[Static Analysis] --> AIReview[AI Review] --> Suggestions --> Report
```

---

Screenshot

```
docs/screenshots/review.png
```

---

# AI Security Scanner

Detects:

- Hardcoded Secrets

- API Keys

- SQL Injection

- XSS

- Authentication Issues

- OWASP Top 10

- Dependency Vulnerabilities

- Weak Encryption

---

Workflow

```mermaid
flowchart TD

Repository --> SecurityAnalysis[Security Analysis] --> RiskDetection[Risk Detection] --> Report
```

---

Screenshot

```
docs/screenshots/security.png
```

---

# Dependency Analysis

Visualizes relationships between packages, modules, and services.

Capabilities:

- Dependency Graph

- Circular Dependency Detection

- Package Analysis

- Import Graph

- Module Relationships

---

Workflow

```mermaid
flowchart LR

Repository --> Imports --> DepGraph[Dependency Graph] --> Visualization
```

---

Screenshot

```
docs/screenshots/dependency.png
```

---

# Repository Analytics Dashboard

Monitor repository health through real-time analytics.

Metrics include:

- Total Files

- Languages

- Commits

- Contributors

- AI Tokens

- Processing Time

- Documentation Coverage

- Security Score

- Code Quality

- Repository Health

---

Workflow

```mermaid
flowchart LR

Repository --> Metrics --> AnalyticsEngine[Analytics Engine] --> Dashboard
```

---

Screenshot

```
docs/screenshots/analytics.png
```

---

# AI Report Export

Export repository insights into professional reports.

Supported Formats

- PDF

- Markdown

- HTML

- JSON

Reports include:

- Documentation

- Code Review

- Security Analysis

- Architecture

- Analytics

---

Workflow

```mermaid
flowchart LR

Analysis --> ReportGen[Report Generator] --> Export --> Formats["PDF / HTML / Markdown"]
```

---

# Feature Summary

| Feature | Status |
|----------|--------|
| AI Repository Chat | Yes |
| GitHub Import | Yes |
| Repository Explorer | Yes |
| Semantic Search | Yes |
| Documentation Generator | Yes |
| README Generator | Yes |
| Architecture Generator | Yes |
| AI Code Review | Yes |
| Security Scanner | Yes |
| Dependency Analysis | Yes |
| Repository Analytics | Yes |
| Report Export | Yes |

---

# Why These Features Matter

DevAssist is more than a chatbot. It is an AI-powered software engineering platform that helps developers:

- Understand repositories faster
- Generate documentation automatically
- Perform semantic code search
- Review code intelligently
- Visualize software architecture
- Detect security issues
- Analyze dependencies
- Monitor repository health
- Produce engineering reports
- Improve developer productivity

---

---

# Technology Stack

DevAssist is built using a modern, scalable, and enterprise-grade technology stack. Each technology has been carefully selected to provide high performance, maintainability, and developer productivity.

---

# Frontend Stack

The frontend provides a modern developer experience with a responsive dashboard, AI chat interface, repository explorer, and analytics.

| Technology | Purpose |
|------------|---------|
| React 19 | Component-based UI |
| TypeScript | Type Safety |
| Vite | Lightning-fast Build Tool |
| Tailwind CSS v4 | Utility-first Styling |
| Zustand | Global State Management |
| React Query | Server State Management |
| Framer Motion | Animations |
| React Router | Client-side Routing |
| Mermaid.js | Architecture Diagrams |
| Lucide Icons | Icon Library |
| React Hook Form | Form Management |
| Zod | Schema Validation |

---

# Backend Stack

The backend exposes REST APIs and coordinates repository processing.

| Technology | Purpose |
|------------|---------|
| Node.js | Runtime |
| Express.js | REST API |
| TypeScript | Type Safety |
| JWT | Authentication |
| Multer | File Uploads |
| Axios | HTTP Requests |
| CORS | Cross-Origin Requests |
| dotenv | Environment Variables |

---

# AI Stack

DevAssist leverages Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) together with Multi-Agent orchestration.

| Technology | Purpose |
|------------|---------|
| LangGraph | Multi-Agent Workflow Engine |
| LangChain | AI Framework |
| Groq SDK | LLM Inference |
| llama-3.3-70b-versatile | Large Language Model |
| Embedding Model | Semantic Embeddings |
| RAG | Repository-aware AI |

---

# Database Stack

| Technology | Purpose |
|------------|---------|
| SQLite | Repository Metadata |
| ChromaDB / Pinecone / FAISS | Vector Database |
| Prisma (Optional) | ORM |

---

# DevOps & Infrastructure

| Technology | Purpose |
|------------|---------|
| Docker | Containerization |
| Docker Compose | Local Deployment |
| GitHub Actions | CI/CD |
| Nginx | Reverse Proxy |
| Render / Railway | Cloud Deployment |
| AWS EC2 | VPS Deployment |

---

# Testing Stack

| Technology | Purpose |
|------------|---------|
| Vitest | Unit Testing |
| React Testing Library | Component Testing |
| Playwright | End-to-End Testing |
| ESLint | Code Quality |
| Prettier | Code Formatting |

---

# Third-Party Integrations

| Service | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| GitHub API | Repository Import |
| Groq API | AI Inference |
| OAuth | Authentication |
| Markdown | Documentation |
| Mermaid | Diagram Generation |

---

# Complete Technology Overview

```text
Frontend
│
├── React 19
├── TypeScript
├── Tailwind CSS
├── Zustand
├── React Query
├── Mermaid
└── Framer Motion

Backend
│
├── Node.js
├── Express
├── JWT
├── Multer
└── Axios

AI
│
├── LangGraph
├── LangChain
├── Groq
├── Llama 3
└── RAG

Database
│
├── SQLite
└── Vector Database

Infrastructure
│
├── Docker
├── GitHub Actions
├── Nginx
└── Cloud Hosting
```

---

# Project Structure

The project follows a modular architecture that separates concerns and promotes maintainability.

```text
devassist/
│
├── dashboard/                     # React Frontend
│
│   ├── components/
│   ├── layouts/
│   ├── pages/
│   ├── hooks/
│   ├── services/
│   ├── stores/
│   ├── utils/
│   ├── styles/
│   └── assets/
│
├── api/                           # Express Backend
│
│   ├── controllers/
│   ├── routes/
│   ├── middlewares/
│   ├── validators/
│   ├── services/
│   ├── repositories/
│   └── config/
│
├── orchestrator/                  # LangGraph
│
│   ├── graph/
│   ├── nodes/
│   ├── workflows/
│   ├── state/
│   └── utils/
│
├── agents/
│
│   ├── documentation/
│   ├── readme/
│   ├── architecture/
│   ├── review/
│   ├── security/
│   ├── dependency/
│   ├── analytics/
│   └── search/
│
├── core/
│
│   ├── github/
│   ├── parser/
│   ├── chunker/
│   ├── embeddings/
│   ├── vectordb/
│   ├── database/
│   ├── config/
│   └── utils/
│
├── docs/
│
│   ├── architecture/
│   ├── diagrams/
│   ├── screenshots/
│   └── reports/
│
├── tests/
│
│   ├── unit/
│   ├── integration/
│   └── e2e/
│
├── docker/
│
├── scripts/
│
├── .github/
│
│   └── workflows/
│
├── .env.example
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── vite.config.ts
├── docker-compose.yml
├── Dockerfile
└── README.md
```

---

# Directory Responsibilities

| Directory | Responsibility |
|------------|---------------|
| dashboard | Frontend UI |
| api | REST APIs |
| orchestrator | LangGraph Execution |
| agents | AI Agents |
| core | Shared Business Logic |
| docs | Documentation |
| tests | Testing |
| docker | Docker Configuration |
| scripts | Automation Scripts |
| .github | GitHub Actions |

---

# Prerequisites

Before running DevAssist, ensure the following software is installed:

- Node.js **22+**
- npm **10+**
- Git
- Docker (Optional)
- Groq API Key
- GitHub Personal Access Token (Optional)

Verify installation:

```bash
node -v
npm -v
git --version
docker --version
```

---

# Environment Variables

Create a `.env` file in the project root.

```env
# Server
NODE_ENV=development
PORT=3000

# Authentication
JWT_SECRET=your_secret_key

# AI
GROQ_API_KEY=gsk_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx

# GitHub
GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx

# Database
DATABASE_URL=sqlite:./devassist.db

# Vector Database
VECTOR_DB=chroma

# Logging
LOG_LEVEL=info
```

---

# Installation

Clone the repository.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/yourusername/devassist.git
```

Move into the project.

```bash
cd devassist
```

Install dependencies.

```bash
npm install
```

Configure environment variables.

```bash
cp .env.example .env
```

Start the development server.

```bash
npm run dev
```

---

# ▶ Quick Start

Run the frontend.

```bash
npm run dev
```

Run the backend.

```bash
npm run server
```

Open your browser.

```
http://localhost:5173
```

Import a repository and begin analyzing your codebase.

---

# Available Scripts

| Command | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| npm run dev | Start Frontend |
| npm run server | Start Backend |
| npm run build | Production Build |
| npm run preview | Preview Production |
| npm run lint | Lint Code |
| npm run test | Run Tests |
| npm run format | Format Code |
| npm run docker | Start Docker Environment |

---

# Development Workflow

```mermaid
flowchart LR

Clone --> Install --> Configure --> Run --> ImportRepo[Import Repository] --> RepoAnalysis[Repository Analysis] --> AIProcessing[AI Processing] --> Dashboard
```

---

# Technology Summary

| Category | Stack |
|----------|-------|
| Frontend | React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS |
| Backend | Node.js, Express |
| AI | LangGraph, LangChain, Groq |
| Database | SQLite |
| Vector Store | Chroma / Pinecone / FAISS |
| Authentication | JWT |
| Deployment | Docker |
| CI/CD | GitHub Actions |
| Testing | Vitest, Playwright |

---
